_comment: Hand-extracted KG supplement for Lens B (B12, 2026-06-21). The nightly graphify build (kg/graph.json, 4,168 nodes, built 2026-06-20 12:01 from the 79-behind working tree) is BLIND to the contextualization publish-gate decision layer: decideBatchReview / decidePublish / buildEntityInsert / buildPublishEntityUpdate all return 0 node hits, and ctx_import_batches returns 0. Only the shared ADR-0017 helper applyHubProposalTransition resolves (1 hit). This file documents, on deploy truth (origin/main@f6a9bec2), the publish-gate call graph the nightly build misses, scoped to the routes B12 audited. NOT a graphify artifact — append-only hand triples per the orchestrator KG rule 5 (do-not-fake clause).
_source_of_truth: origin/main (git show), NOT working tree (80 behind at audit time)
_audit: B12 — Lens B Hub functional readiness — contextualization functional half

nodes.test_coverage: NONE (no route test, no shadow test; decision logic only via lib/approval.test.ts)
nodes.note: ADR-0017 publish gate: proposed->verified. The single most consequential write in the HubV3 contextualization surface.
sessionOrDemo (demo-accessible) — wider than the sessionOr401 siblings; verify demo tenant cannot read another tenant's asset context.

nodes.test_coverage: lib/approval.test.ts + acceptance-matrix.test.ts (15+13 cases) — logic tested directly (good pattern)
lib unit only — never exercised through the route handler
lib unit only — never exercised through the route handler

nodes.note: ADR-0017 helper — the ONLY publish-gate dependency the nightly graph resolves. Shared spine visible; ctx-local decision layer invisible.
publish target: proposed->verified insert/update, no-overwrite guard on verified/deprecated
lockstep projection: pending->accepted via applyHubProposalTransition (ADR-0017), keyed on extracted_data->>'ctx_extraction_id'
source of publishable rows: status='accepted' AND uns_path_proposed IS NOT NULL
join key import_batch_id ties extractions to a batch

insight: The publish gate's dependency graph splits exactly along the test-coverage line: the one dependency the nightly graphify build can see (applyHubProposalTransition, the shared ADR-0017 spine) is the part already proven sound by A12/A13; the four ctx-local decision functions it can't see (decideBatchReview/decidePublish/buildEntityInsert/buildPublishEntityUpdate) are the half whose ROUTE-LEVEL wiring is test-orphaned. Same present-target/missing-newest fingerprint A10/B11/E10/F11/A13 keep surfacing — now localized to the publish gate's route handler.
